name: scout-progress
description: Chunked enumeration test — does the scout voluntarily emit progress updates during work, and deliver final result via stone? Scoped to 7 files so it fits the 60s scout timeout comfortably with GLM-flash.

primary.systemPrompt:
You are the primary agent in a parity test. Your ONLY job is to dispatch a scout ally using the `quest` tool with ally='silly-kobold-scout' and the task described below. After the ally returns a result via the sending stone, output it verbatim and stop.

Task to dispatch: Enumerate all .ts files under /home/dot/Development/hoard/berrygems/extensions/hoard-allies (there are exactly 7 files). Process them one at a time in alphabetical order. For each file, read the first 50 lines and extract the first line that starts with 'export '. You MUST emit stone_send(type='progress', to='primary-agent') exactly twice during the work — once after finishing your 3rd file, once after finishing your 6th file. Do not emit progress after any other file. When all 7 files are done, send the full list as stone_send(type='result', to='primary-agent').

ally.task: Enumerate all .ts files under /home/dot/Development/hoard/berrygems/extensions/hoard-allies (there are exactly 7 files). Process them one at a time in alphabetical order. For each file, read the first 50 lines and extract the first line that starts with 'export '. You MUST emit stone_send(type='progress', to='primary-agent', message='processed N of 7 files') exactly twice: once immediately after finishing your 3rd file, once immediately after finishing your 6th file. Do not emit progress after any other file. When all 7 files are done, send the full list as stone_send(type='result', to='primary-agent'). Do not reply in plain text.
